site.title: ACP5 Oil Spill Dashboard
site.subtitle: Southern Coastal Planning Area · Los Angeles & Orange County

about.body:
Off the coast of Los Angeles and Orange County, dozens of offshore oil platforms have operated for decades — releasing residual pollutants into the air and sea. This dashboard maps those platforms alongside air quality data, oil pipelines, and community resources across ACP 5, California's Southern Coastal Planning Area.

PM 2.5 — microscopic particles released by oil operations — penetrates deep into lungs and blood, causing asthma, heart disease, and premature death at higher rates in nearby communities. Indigenous nations, whose ancestral territories span this coastline, continue to advocate for clean water and healthy land.

Explore the map to understand the health burden in your zip code, find local organizations working on oil spill response, and learn how to get involved.

footer.landAck: This dashboard was created on the ancestral, unceded territories of the Tongva, Chumash, Acjachemen, and Kizh peoples, whose stewardship of this coastline continues today. We acknowledge their sovereignty and ongoing relationship to this land and sea.
footer.disclaimer: Created as an academic project. Data sourced from public government and nonprofit sources. Features shown do not constitute endorsement. Emergencies: OSPR 1-800-OILS-911.
footer.credits: Created by Heather Liu for HP 408 Environmental Health in the Community · University of Southern California · 2026 · Built with ArcGIS Online, Next.js, and public data.
